title: King's Edge deliverable benefit schema
description: Canonical authoring schema for deliverable-level benefits. Benefits describe value and the future state to be realised. Measures are authored once at deliverable level and linked back to benefits through supportsBenefits.

stepId.description: ID of the delivery timeline step that produces or enables the thing being referenced.

outputId.description: Optional ID of a step output when the output has a stable authored ID.

outputTitle.description: Optional title of a step output when no stable output ID exists yet.

note.description: Optional explanation of how this step or output enables the benefit.

statement.description: Short statement of the value created when the deliverable is used. This should not be a product, task or activity description.

beneficiary.description: Who benefits from the value being realised.

benefitType.description: Broad type of value, for example articulation, evidence, improvement, institutional learning or responsible use.

realisationPeriod.description: When the value is expected to begin or mature.

successLooksLike.description: Concise, outcome-oriented description of the future state if the benefit is being realised.

enabledBy.description: Traceability references back to outputs or steps already defined in the delivery timeline. Do not define new products here.

linkedOutputs.description: Legacy alias for enabledBy. New authoring should use enabledBy.

evidenceOfSuccess.description: Deprecated authored evidence list. The app should derive evidence of success from measures linked through supportsBenefits.

measures.description: Deprecated nested measure list. Author measures once at deliverable level and link them with supportsBenefits.

examples.title: Programmes can claim their graduate futures value with confidence
examples.statement: Programme teams can make stronger, more nuanced and evidence-informed claims about the futures their programme makes possible for students.
examples.beneficiary: Programme teams, departments, students, applicants, recruitment teams and faculties
examples.successLooksLike: Programme-level graduate futures stories are confident, evidence-linked, academically owned and appropriately caveated.

enabledBy.outputTitle: First working graduate futures evidence pack
